feature: Anti-pattern reference catalog with evaluator integration
description: Port four anti-pattern reference docs from obra/superpowers (MIT) into .bober/anti-patterns/ with inline attribution. Update agents/bober-evaluator.md to require citing anti-pattern names by handle when flagging regressions or failed criteria.

successCriteria.description: .bober/anti-patterns/README.md exists as a table-of-contents indexing all four ported docs with short (1-line) summaries each. Includes a top-level attribution block citing obra/superpowers (MIT license) as the source.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read .bober/anti-patterns/README.md. Verify it lists all four files. Verify attribution block exists.

successCriteria.description: .bober/anti-patterns/testing-anti-patterns.md exists with substantive content (>=80 lines), opens with MIT attribution to obra/superpowers, and documents at minimum the anti-patterns from /tmp/superpowers/skills/test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read .bober/anti-patterns/testing-anti-patterns.md. wc -l file. Grep for 'obra/superpowers' and 'MIT'. Spot-check that named anti-patterns from the source file are preserved.

successCriteria.description: .bober/anti-patterns/condition-based-waiting.md exists with substantive content (>=60 lines), opens with MIT attribution, and reflects /tmp/superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/condition-based-waiting.md.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the file. wc -l. Grep attribution. Verify the file documents the condition-based-waiting pattern.

successCriteria.description: .bober/anti-patterns/root-cause-tracing.md exists with substantive content (>=60 lines), opens with MIT attribution, reflects /tmp/superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/root-cause-tracing.md.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the file. wc -l. Grep attribution. Spot-check content correspondence.

successCriteria.description: .bober/anti-patterns/defense-in-depth.md exists with substantive content (>=40 lines), opens with MIT attribution, reflects /tmp/superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/defense-in-depth.md.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the file. wc -l. Grep attribution. Spot-check correspondence.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-evaluator.md instructs the evaluator to reference anti-pattern names from .bober/anti-patterns/ in the regressions field of EvalResult when applicable. The instruction names the catalog path and shows an example regression-entry with an anti-pattern citation.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read agents/bober-evaluator.md. Grep for '.bober/anti-patterns'. Verify the instruction and example exist.

expectedChanges.description: Index of ported anti-pattern docs with MIT attribution
Add anti-pattern citation requirement and example to regressions reporting

generatorNotes: The four source files are already on disk at /tmp/superpowers/skills/test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md and /tmp/superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/{condition-based-waiting.md,root-cause-tracing.md,defense-in-depth.md}. Read each one and port. Verbatim port is permitted by MIT — the requirement is the attribution header on each file. Attribution template (top of every file): '> Ported from [obra/superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) — MIT License. Original: skills/<path>/<file>.md.' Light editing is acceptable for: (a) replacing 'your human partner' with 'the user' or 'the orchestrator', (b) replacing superpowers-specific skill cross-references with bober skill names where the bober equivalent exists, (c) trimming examples that reference superpowers-only files. Do NOT rewrite the empirical content (Red Flags tables, anti-pattern catalogs) — that's the tuned behavior-shaping content per AGENTS.md of the source. README.md should be ~30-50 lines: top-level attribution, then a markdown table with columns | Anti-pattern | When to flag | File |. The evaluator update should be a small addition (~10-15 lines) in the regressions-reporting section: '## Anti-Pattern Citations' subsection or a bullet point in existing reporting guidance, with example: '{name: "asymmetric-assertions", source: ".bober/anti-patterns/testing-anti-patterns.md", evidence: "src/foo.test.ts:42-50"}'.
evaluatorNotes: Verify each anti-pattern file actually contains substantive content, not stub headers — line counts in success criteria are minimums; check the body. Verify the attribution block on every ported file (not just README) — MIT requires attribution alongside the work. Verify the evaluator's new citation instruction is testable: the example regression entry must be parseable JSON (or a clear JSON shape if it's documented as a TypeScript-like type). Reject if any ported file omits attribution. Reject if the evaluator changes contradict the existing regressions schema (regressions is currently an array — additions go INSIDE entries, not as a new top-level field, unless the schema is intentionally extended).
